para_trans: 2. Separate order for discharge of transferred officers in return.
There's no need. Therefore, as soon as the transfer order is received, the concerned shall be relieved forthwith.
Be at the location of the transfer.
3. In any case, after the transfer, the salary of the officers who have been employed on account of the transfer.
The transfer should not be done from the previous office.
4. Transfer of charge immediately after the officer concerned appears for transfer.
Their Appointment Report along with the certificate prescribed by the Commissioner, Animal Husbandry, State of Maharashtra, Pune.
The route should be submitted to the government without any delay.
5. Maharashtra Civil Service (Retirement) on superannuation of transferred officers.
period) to the place of transfer within the prescribed period as prescribed by the rules in the Rules, 1981.
Be careful what happens. Otherwise, their period of absence is as a "non-deceiving day."
The concerned should note that it will be counted.
6. The head of the office concerned shall forthwith appoint the officers concerned to the post of transferor.
If not released, the responsibility lies entirely with the concerned Regional Aided Animal Husbandry.
The Commissioner, Animal Husbandry, Pune is informed against the officers who have not been relieved of their duties.
Disciplinary action should be proposed and reported to the government by e-mailing desk.adf-1@mah.gov.in.
Submit to the mail.

para_trans: 7. grant leave to the officer concerned for any reason without the prior approval of the Government.
No. Also, as per Rule-30 (c) of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Leave) Rules, 1981, the original office
No leave applications of transferred officers shall be entertained or considered.
No. If such applications are received, they are returned to the concerned authorities by post at the original address.
should do.
8. Relating to the transfer of officers to the place of posting at the place of the revised transfer.
If attempts are made to pressure the administration through political persons or other office bearers,
This is in violation of Rule 23 of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Discipline and Appeal) Rules, 1979.
Being the doer, disciplinary action will be taken against them assuming it is misconduct,
This should be noted by the concerned.
The Government Order is available on the Government of Maharashtra website www.maharashtra.gov.in.
